SAS's extended medevac partnership demonstrates sustained commercial aviation capacity for military medical operations, securing long-term revenue while supporting critical humanitarian logistics.

Employee-designed livery demonstrates how internal engagement can amplify brand heritage narratives while strengthening organizational identity during milestone celebrations.

Drones Mar 27, 2026

The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains

The Earth observation sector is experiencing a golden age. Satellite hardware and launch costs have plummeted, and computer power, driven by AI and analytics, continues to improve. That’s led to a genuine revolution in precision agriculture and automated supply chain monitoring. For agribusinesses,...

Satellite data limitations in agricultural supply chain monitoring create opportunities for aviation-based solutions like drones and manned aircraft to provide real-time ground validation.

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document why selected risk controls are proportionate to identified hazards, creating an auditable justification for residual risk acceptance decisions.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

La Guardia Crash: NTSB Releases Footage & Additional Info Regarding Tragedy

You are probably well-aware by now that an Air Canada Express Bombardier CRJ900 crashed into a fire truck at La Guardia airport on the night of March 22nd, 2026. While, on the surface, the tragic accident that took the lives of the two aircraft pilots looks to be a straightforward case of...

NTSB investigation footage reveals the La Guardia CRJ900 crash involved ground vehicle interaction, suggesting runway incursion protocols may require enhanced coordination between air and ground operations.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion

NASA’s proposed changes to its support of commercial space stations have created concern and confusion among companies developing them, the head of an industry organization warned.

Industry uncertainty over NASA's shifting commercial station support threatens timeline momentum and investor confidence in emerging space station ventures.

Heathrow's sustained influx of new long-haul carriers signals sustained capacity recovery and competitive route expansion post-pandemic, justifying recent terminal investments.

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Organizations with strong psychological safety cultures report 5-10x more safety issues, enabling proactive hazard identification rather than reactive crisis management in SMS programs.

General Aviation Mar 27, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like CID serve as critical training hubs for pilots while simultaneously accommodating commercial operations, creating operational complexity that demands sophisticated airspace management.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Boeing Boost: Major African Carrier Doubles Down On 737 MAX With 10 New Jets

The new aircraft will come quicker than you might expect.

Accelerated 737 MAX deliveries to African operators signal manufacturer confidence in production ramp-up and growing regional demand for modern narrow-body capacity.

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit

SAN FRANCISCO – Canadian startup SBQuantum plans to send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit March 30 on a Spire Global satellite flying on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare. Spire is providing the satellite, ground stations and data processing for SBQuantum’s magnetometer, which is...

Quantum sensing technology deployment in LEO will enable unprecedented precision magnetic field measurements for space weather monitoring and aviation navigation applications.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service

Japanese company ispace is revising its lunar lander design and further delaying the first mission by its American subsidiary while also unveiling plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

ispace's pivot toward lunar infrastructure services signals a shift from one-time lander missions to recurring revenue through orbital communications, addressing a critical gap in sustained lunar operations.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 27, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrated operational optimization across airlines, airports, and airspace management offers the most immediate pathway to accommodate projected traffic growth while addressing environmental pressures before technological breakthroughs reach scale.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Qantas Scraps Nonstop Perth-Paris Route In Major European Network Shake-Up

Qantas shakes up its international network to offer more flights to Europe via Singapore.

Qantas is pivoting from direct ultra-long-haul routes to a hub-and-spoke model through Singapore, prioritizing frequency and connectivity over nonstop convenience to capture more passengers.

See Simple Flying →

Lockheed Martin F-35 workers open contract talks in Fort Worth

The union representing around 5,000 workers building the F-35 at Lockheed Martin’s Fort Worth, Texas, plant has opened…...

Labor negotiations at a key F-35 production facility could impact defense contractor supply chain stability and aircraft delivery timelines across allied nations.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The MD-11 & DC-10

Though closely related, the MD-11 and DC-10 differed in range, cockpit design, aerodynamics, and ultimately commercial success.

The MD-11's improved aerodynamics and extended range couldn't overcome the aircraft's handling challenges and cockpit complexity, limiting its commercial appeal despite superior technical specifications.

See Simple Flying →

Integrating specialized EW payloads onto commercial drone platforms expands tactical reconnaissance capabilities for defense and intelligence operations beyond traditional surveillance roles.

DJI Avata 360 global launch begins, US delayed again

DJI has kicked off the global rollout of the Avata 360, positioning it as its most immersive consumer drone yet. In many parts of the world, that means orders are either already live or pre-sales have begun. In the US, though, things are playing out a little differently, again. more…...

DJI's continued US market delays suggest regulatory scrutiny may be fragmenting the global consumer drone market into separate rollout schedules.

Some of the country's busiest domestic routes are operated by widebodies.

Widebody deployment on domestic routes indicates carriers are optimizing premium cabin capacity on high-yield flights rather than purely maximizing seat counts.

Satellite Spots a Spawn

The activity of herring around Vancouver Island in British Columbia brightened coastal waters enough to be detectable from space.

Satellite remote sensing technology's sensitivity to ocean bioluminescence demonstrates applications beyond traditional Earth observation, expanding aerospace capabilities for marine ecosystem monitoring.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Range The Airbus A350-1000 Has Compared To The Boeing 777-300ER

The Airbus A350-1000 and Boeing 777-300ER are long-haul aviation titans, but the A350's slight edge in range may not be as decisive as it seems.

While the A350-1000's superior range enables slightly longer routes, the 777-300ER's established global infrastructure and proven reliability may deliver greater operational value than marginal distance advantages.

Here’s How Much Larger The GE9X Is Compared To The GE90

The engine is set to enter commercial service on the upcoming Boeing 777X.

The GE9X's significant size increase over its GE90 predecessor enables the 777X to achieve greater fuel efficiency and payload capacity despite regulatory constraints on engine diameter.

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 drone launch delayed in US

DJI’s 360° drone launch may already be underway globally, but US buyers could be stuck waiting. A newly updated banner on DJI’s official Amazon US storefront now points to March 30 at 8 a.m. EST as the release timing for its upcoming DJI Avata 360 drone, subtly overriding the March 26 worldwide...

DJI's US market launch delays suggest continued regulatory scrutiny of Chinese drone manufacturers despite global availability.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft

Commercial space tracking data shows U.S. satellites coordinating maneuvers to maintain proximity and continuous observation of a pair of Chinese spacecraft in geostationary orbit.

U.S. orbital surveillance capabilities now demonstrate coordinated multi-satellite tracking of foreign GEO assets, signaling advanced space situational awareness that could inform future airspace security protocols.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Space Command begins phased move to Alabama

The new headquarters building is expected to be completed by 2031...

U.S. Space Command's relocation to Alabama signals a strategic shift in aerospace defense infrastructure, with workforce transitions spanning nearly a decade affecting recruitment, retention, and regional economic development.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA

Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

April 2026 offers optimal viewing conditions for celestial events that aviation crews should monitor for potential atmospheric visibility impacts on night operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa

Se invita a los medios de comunicación el martes 21 de abril al Centro de Vuelo Espacial Goddard de la NASA en Greenbelt, Maryland, para conocer el telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la agencia, cuya construcción terminó recientemente y el cual se encuentra ultimando las pruebas previas a su...

NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has completed construction and is entering final pre-launch testing, positioning it as a critical successor to Hubble for deep space observation missions.

See NASA →
